page: Amazon Web Services m6id.large is a general-purpose virtual machine instance powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features an Intel Xeon 8375C processor with an x86_64 architecture, providing 2 vCPUs mapped to 1 physical core with hyper-threading enabled. The CPU operates at a clock speed of 3.5 GHz and includes a 54 MB L3 cache. The instance is equipped with 8.0 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz, translating to 4.0 GB of RAM per physical core.

A key hardware feature of the m6id.large is its bundled local storage, consisting of a 118 GB NVMe SSD instance store. This local storage provides low-latency, high-speed ephemeral disk access. For network connectivity, the instance offers a baseline network bandwidth of 1 Gbps. It does not include any GPU hardware.

Performance benchmarks reveal a clear distinction between single-core and multi-core capabilities. Single-core performance is moderate, with stress-ng single-core and Geekbench single-core scores falling into the average category. However, multi-core benchmarks, such as Geekbench multi-core and stress-ng multi-core, are rated as poor, which is expected given the physical limitation of a single core. Memory bandwidth tests for both cached and uncached reads and writes also rank in the lower percentiles.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the m6id.large offers a balanced entry-level option for workloads requiring local NVMe storage without the overhead of larger instance sizes. It is well-suited for general-purpose applications, lightweight web serving, basic text processing, and development environments that benefit from fast local temporary storage but do not require high multi-threaded compute or massive memory bandwidth.
description: Amazon Web Services m6id.large is a general-purpose instance powered by an Intel Xeon 8375C processor running at 3.5 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture, it provides 2 vCPUs across 1 physical core, paired with 8.0 GB of DDR4 RAM. A key differentiator is its integrated 118 GB NVMe SSD local instance store, which delivers low-latency ephemeral storage, alongside a baseline network bandwidth of 1 Gbps. Benchmark data indicates average single-core CPU and text processing performance, though multi-core and memory bandwidth metrics are relatively low due to the single-core hardware constraint. This instance is qualitatively cost-effective for lightweight workloads requiring fast local storage, such as basic web serving, development environments, and simple text processing tasks, where high multi-threaded performance is not a primary requirement.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m6id.large is a general-purpose instance featuring 2 vCPUs, 8.0 GB DDR4 RAM, and a 118 GB NVMe SSD instance store powered by an Intel Xeon 3.5 GHz processor.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m6id.large is a general-purpose instance with 2 vCPUs, 8 GB RAM, and a 118 GB NVMe SSD, powered by an Intel Xeon CPU.
tagline: An entry-level general-purpose instance featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processing and integrated high-speed NVMe local SSD storage.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 8375C processor with 2 vCPUs and 3.5 GHz clock speed
8.0 GB of DDR4 memory at 3 GHz
118 GB of local NVMe SSD instance storage
Suitable for lightweight web serving and development environments
